question.text: What transaction tax applies to commodity derivative transactions and how does it compare to STT on equity derivatives?

answer.text: CTT (Commodity Transaction Tax) at 0.01% on seller of commodity options — similar to STT in concept but different rates and applicability compared to equity derivatives STT of 0.02% on futures and 0.10% on options
CTT applies at the same rate as equity STT (0.02% on futures, 0.10% on options)
No transaction tax applies to commodity derivatives — only equity derivatives attract STT
CTT applies at 0.05% on both buyer and seller of all commodity derivative transactions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CTT was introduced for commodity derivatives (non-agricultural) — it's similar in concept to STT but with different rates and scope. STT applies to equity derivatives (0.02% futures seller, 0.10% options on exercise). Neither STT nor CTT applies to currency or interest rate derivatives. **The Trap:** The rates and applicability differ between CTT and STT. Currency and IR derivatives are exempt from both. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is a XIII-unique cross-asset comparison. Series VIII covers STT, Series XVI covers CTT, Series I confirms no transaction tax on currency derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The absence of STT/CTT on currency and interest rate derivatives makes them relatively more cost-efficient for high-frequency trading compared to equity and commodity derivatives.
